description: Trauma assessment and management for a motor vehicle collision victim
system_instruction: Focus on ATLS primary and secondary survey, trauma resuscitation, and team coordination.

case_metadata.title: Blunt Trauma from Motor Vehicle Collision

patient_persona.name: Michael Torres
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Involved in high-speed motor vehicle collision 30 minutes ago
patient_persona.background_story: Front-end collision at approximately 60mph, airbag deployed, prolonged extrication required. Complaining of chest pain and abdominal pain

initial_prompt: I was in a bad car accident. My chest hurts where I hit the steering wheel and my belly is starting to hurt too. I can't take a deep breath.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Blunt chest trauma with pulmonary contusion and splenic laceration

history_of_presenting_illness.location: Chest and left upper quadrant abdomen
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Sharp chest pain worsened by breathing, dull abdominal pain

primary_survey_findings.A_airway: Patent, speaking in full sentences, no obstruction
primary_survey_findings.B_breathing: Tachypneic, decreased breath sounds left base, chest wall contusion, no flail segment, trachea midline
primary_survey_findings.C_circulation: Tachycardia, mild hypotension, skin cool and moist, cap refill 3 seconds, no active external hemorrhage
primary_survey_findings.D_disability: GCS 15, pupils equal and reactive, moving all extremities
primary_survey_findings.E_exposure: Multiple abrasions and contusions on chest and abdomen, seatbelt sign across chest and abdomen

imaging_findings.chest_xray: Left pulmonary contusion, no pneumothorax, no hemothorax, no rib fracture seen
imaging_findings.focused_abdominal_sonography_in_trauma: Positive for free fluid in splenorenal recess
imaging_findings.CT_chest_abdomen_pelvis: Left pulmonary contusion, Grade II splenic laceration with moderate hemoperitoneum, no hollow viscus injury, no spinal injury

fas_scan_results: Positive - free fluid in left upper quadrant

end_session.patient_response: Am I going to be okay? Thank you for taking care of me.

critical_issue.response: Patient's blood pressure dropping. Hemorrhagic shock protocol and blood transfusion required immediately.

evaluation_criteria.Primary_Survey: Systematic ATLS primary survey with appropriate interventions at each step
evaluation_criteria.Resuscitation: Appropriate fluid resuscitation and activation of massive transfusion protocol if indicated
evaluation_criteria.Secondary_Survey: Complete secondary survey with appropriate adjuncts (FAST, X-rays, CT)
evaluation_criteria.Injury_Identification: Accurate identification and prioritization of life-threatening injuries
evaluation_criteria.Team_Leadership: Effective trauma team leadership and communication
evaluation_criteria.Disposition_Decision: Appropriate decision regarding OR vs ICU vs transfer to higher level of care

system_instruction: Focus on early recognition of sepsis, source control, and goal-directed therapy using Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines.

case_metadata.title: Septic Shock from Urinary Tract Infection

patient_persona.name: Dorothy Williams
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Confusion, fever, and weakness for 2 days
patient_persona.background_story: Brought in by daughter who found her confused and febrile. History of recurrent UTIs. Has been feeling 'off' for 2 days with burning on urination

initial_prompt: My mom is very confused today and has a high fever. She's been saying urination burns for a few days and now she can barely stand up.

history_of_presenting_illness.character: Dysuria initially, now fever, confusion, weakness

physical_exam_findings.abdomen: Suprapubic tenderness, no rebound or guarding
physical_exam_findings.neurological: Confused, oriented to person only, no focal deficits

lab_results.urinalysis: Cloudy, positive for leukocyte esterase, nitrites, WBC >50/hpf, bacteria

imaging_findings.renal_ultrasound: No hydronephrosis, normal kidney size, no abscess

sepsis_assessment.sepsis_bundle: Sepsis with septic shock - immediate 1-hour bundle initiation required

end_session.patient_response: My daughter told me I had a serious infection. Thank you for treating me quickly.

critical_issue.response: Patient becoming increasingly hypotensive despite fluids. Vasopressor initiation required.

evaluation_criteria.Sepsis_Screening: Rapid recognition of sepsis using qSOFA/SIRS criteria
evaluation_criteria.Resuscitation: Appropriate 1-hour sepsis bundle completion including lactate, blood cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, vasopressors
evaluation_criteria.Antibiotic_Selection: Appropriate empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics considering allergies and renal function
evaluation_criteria.Source_Control: Identification and management of infection source
evaluation_criteria.Hemodynamic_Monitoring: Appropriate fluid resuscitation and vasopressor management with hemodynamic monitoring
evaluation_criteria.Lactate_Clearance: Appropriate monitoring of lactate clearance as marker of resuscitation adequacy
